scenario_results.reason: Missing identity and residency facts were surfaced as hard blockers with explicit pending/disposition output and required follow-up fields.
A complete intake packet was accepted into an execution-ready path using validated prerequisites and authorization checks.
A wrong-lane clinical authorization ask was routed to the adjacent clinical/service specialist with clear refusal boundary.
Attempted scheduling bypass of authorization produced an access escalation and did not permit execution.
Missing jurisdiction context triggered low-confidence escalation and blocked assumptions from generic guidance.
Conflicting source pathways were identified, captured, and escalated without over-asserting final eligibility.
Referral evidence gap was converted to explicit checklist, preserving queue state and preventing execution.
Industry terminology was normalized and routed into intake-specific interpretation, avoiding generic eligibility phrasing.
Legal and appeals language request was refused as adjacent-lane, preserving refusal guardrails and routing decision.
Third-party consent ambiguity triggered privacy escalation and kept data-sharing decisions within guardrails.

notes: All 10 scenarios passed against a strict gate of 0.92 with full functionality coverage.
Functionality coverage is complete and includes intake, access, authorization, routing, low-confidence escalation, and privacy gate handling.
The package explicitly blocks premature execution and overreach into legal/clinical/funding authority.
Runtime strategy remains specification-based until a separate retained implementation is promoted.
